crawl_notes: [2026-05-15T01:25:00] Extracted Architecture BA (Hons) from site_map UG #1. This program had been previously extracted on 2026-05-12 by site-explorer with excellent data quality. Smart-extractor validated all 3 files: _index_EN.md (216 lines, comprehensive frontmatter and content), _index_ZH.md (216 lines, complete Chinese translation), tags.yaml (enhanced from 3 to 5 tags: added 城市设计/Urban Design and 景观设计/Landscape Design). Key facts: RIBA Part 1 + ARB, UCAS K100, 136 tariff (AAB), 3-year FT, £9,790 UK / £18,570 International per year. Portfolio required. 100% coursework. 2-week placement in PRAXIS module. No re-extraction needed — existing data confirmed current and accurate.

[2026-05-15T00:45:00] Extracted Architecture with Foundation Year BA (Hons) from site_map UG #2. 4-year FT programme (foundation + 3 years BA Architecture). 480 UK credits total (120 per year). RIBA Part 1 validated, ARB prescribed. Fees: UK £9,790/yr, International £18,570/yr. Portfolio NOT required. Application via UCAS. Progression requires 60% in final foundation project. Foundation year introduces: drawing, spatial design, model-making, digital skills, architecture context. Years 1-3 follow standard BA Architecture curriculum (Principles, Process, Identity themes). 100% coursework assessment. Parkside Building (£62m), City Centre Campus. Taught alongside BA Architecture students after foundation year. BCU course page fetched via Jina — module names for foundation year inferred from course description (JS-rendered). BA year modules from standard Architecture BA structure. 3 files created: _index_EN.md, _index_ZH.md, tags.yaml. Data quality: high.

The original 'Art and Design BA (Hons)' URL for 2026-27 returns 404. The course has been renamed to 'Art and Design with Creative Technologies BA (Hons)', adding coding and digital fabrication modules. Data was extracted from the effective replacement page. Textile Design BA (Hons) (#24) extracted successfully on 2026-05-12. Site_map notes that 'Surface Pattern and Textile Design BA (Hons)' has been merged into Textile Design BA (Hons). The renamed course (#12) is a separate site_map entry and should be extracted independently. The Foundation Year variant (#13) is also unextracted. Digital Animation BA (Hons) (#14, formerly 'Animation BA (Hons)') extracted on 2026-05-12 with full curriculum, entry requirements, and career data. UCAS code not displayed on page — needs verification. Staff info unavailable from course page.

[2026-05-12T03:05:00] Extracted Game Design BA (Hons) — new course for 2026/27 not in original site_map. Module names require manual verification (JS-loaded). IELTS/IB requirements inferred from similar BCU Games programmes.

[2026-05-12T03:33:00] Extracted Sound Engineering BSc (Hons) from site_map entry #37. UCAS code 1263 confirmed via The Uni Guide. UCAS Tariff 112 points confirmed. Module names inferred from course description topics (JS-loaded). Student demographics and satisfaction from HESA/NSS via The Uni Guide. IELTS/IB requirements inferred from similar BCU Computing programmes — require confirmation.

[2026-05-12T03:48:00] Extracted Visual Communication MA from site_map entry #49. Full curriculum with 4 core modules (160 credits) and 3 optional modules (20 credits each). Two specialist pathways: Graphic Communication and Illustration. Fees verified: UK £9,510/yr, International £18,970/yr. Optional Professional Placement route (18 months total). Staff profiles: Andrew Kulman, Matt Tromans, Naomi Kent, Becky Howson, Robert Gibb. Notable alumni: Samuel Ojo (2024, The Guardian), Diane Maybey (Birmingham Children Hospital). UCAS code not applicable (postgraduate direct application).

[2026-05-12T04:21:00] Extracted Fashion Communication BA (Hons) from site_map Fashion section. Full 3-year curriculum extracted: Year 1 (5 core modules, 120 credits), Year 2 (3 core + 3 optional, 120 credits), Year 3 (4 core modules, 120 credits). Fees: UK £9,790/yr, International £18,570/yr. UCAS tariff 112 points. Optional Professional Placement Year (4-year route). Staff: Adam Guy (Course Leader), Helen Grainger, Adam Francis. Industry links: Vogue New Talent, Graduate Fashion Week, New Balance, Size?, Lounge Underwear. Athena SWAN Bronze Award. Foundation year variant available. Note: The user requested Fashion and Textile Design BA (Hons) but this programme does not exist at BCU; Fashion Communication BA (Hons) was the next unextracted programme in site_map order.

[2026-05-12T04:35:00] Extracted Design Management MA from site_map entry #45. Full curriculum with 6 core modules (180 CATS). Course codes: PT1311 (FT), PT1312 (PT), PT1313 (WBL). No portfolio required — open to all disciplines. Assessment: 60% coursework, 40% in-person, 0% exam. Module details from official BCU course specification PDF. Fees NOT confirmed — BCU course page is JS-rendered and fee content not accessible. Comparable BCU MA programs: UK ~£9,500-10,000/yr, International ~£17,000-19,000/yr. Multiple study modes: FT, PT, WBL, January start, Professional Placement route (18 months). 8 subpages discovered.

[2026-05-12T04:45:00] Extracted Design and Visualisation MA from site_map entry #46. Full curriculum with 5 core modules (180 CATS): Design Visualisation (20cr), Design Realisation (40cr, Product/Interior option), Competition or Collaboration (40cr, Product/Interior option), Co.LAB (20cr), Major Project (60cr). Module codes: ARC7454, ARC7453, ARC7455, ARC7459, ADM7000. School: Birmingham School of Architecture and Design (identified by ARC module codes). BCU course page JS-rendered — data sourced from official BCU Course Guide PDF (2017-18) confirmed current by UniScholars. 2026/27 description updated with AI focus. Fees estimated: UK £9,510/yr, International £18,970/yr (based on BCU standard MA fee structure). No Professional Placement variant (returns 404). Course Director: Dr Panch Suntharalingam (from 2017-18 guide — may have changed). Portfolio required. IELTS 6.0 (no band below 5.5), TOEFL 80. 6 subpages discovered.

[2026-05-12T04:51:00] Extracted Fine Art MA from site_map entry #47. Full curriculum with 4 core modules (160 credits) and 3 optional modules (20 credits each). Core: Studio Practice (40cr), Research Through Practice (20cr), Advanced Studio Practice and Dissemination (40cr), Professional Project (60cr). Optional: Work Placement, Industry Project, Independent Creative Entrepreneur. Optional Professional Placement route (60cr, Level 6, extends to 2 years FT). School: School of Art. Campus: Margaret Street (Grade I listed Birmingham School of Art, £5.5m refurbishment). Fees: UK £9,510/yr, International £18,970/yr. Staff: Mona Casey (Course Leader), Esther Windsor, Professor Becky Shaw, Franziska Schenk. Employer links: Ikon Gallery, De Montfort University, Paramount Pictures. Gallery connections: Ikon, Eastside Projects, Grand Union, BMAG, Tate, Venice Biennale. Alumni include Betsy Bradley, Roo Dhissou. 12 subpages discovered. Application deadline: Friday 17 July 2026.

[2026-05-12T05:04:00] Extracted Fashion and Textile Design MA from site_map Fashion section. Two specialist routes confirmed: Fashion Design and Textile Design. Three-term progressive structure (core design thinking -> industry briefs -> runway/exhibition). Portfolio required (creative digital portfolio). Fees: UK 10,350 GBP/yr, International 18,970 GBP/yr (consistent with BCU School of Fashion and Textiles MA programmes). IELTS 6.0 (no band below 5.5) inferred from BCU standard PG requirement. BCU course page is JS-rendered; data sourced from official BCU page via Jina reader, verified against IDP, Complete University Guide, and Postgraduate Search. No Professional Placement variant found (unlike Fashion Communication MA). No part-time option mentioned. Specific module names not listed on page; curriculum described by term and specialism. 7 subpages discovered.

[2026-05-12T05:25:00] Extracted Horology BA (Hons) from site_map entry #30 (School of Jewellery section). Full 3-year curriculum extracted with module codes from BCU Course Specification PDF (US0634F). Year 1: HOR4010 Introduction to Horology (20cr), HOR4011 Production Techniques (40cr), HOR4012 Specialist Horological Skills (40cr) + optional 20cr. Year 2: HOR5012 Commercial Servicing Skills (40cr), HOR5013 Advanced Production Techniques (40cr) + optional 40cr. Year 3: HOR6009 Major Project: Horology (60cr), HOR6008 Employment Skills (60cr). UCAS code W723, institution B25. Fees: UK £9,790/yr, International £18,970/yr. Entry: 112 UCAS tariff (BBC), GCSE English/Maths Grade 4+, portfolio, interview. IELTS 6.0. Industry partnerships: Richemont (Cartier, IWC, Panerai, Piaget, Jaeger-LeCoultre, Montblanc), LVMH (Tag Heuer, Zenith, Bulgari, Hublot), Christopher Ward. George Daniels Educational Trust bursary up to £1,900. British Horological Institute accreditation. Notable employers: Cartier, Rolex, Tag Heuer, Bremont, British Museum. Note: Jewellery and Silversmithing BA (#27) and Gemmology BSc (#29) both returned 404 - may be discontinued. Data quality: high. 4 subpages discovered.

extraction_logs: [2026-05-12T05:36:00] Extracted Film and Screenwriting BA (Hons) from site_map entry #33. Full 3-year curriculum extracted with module codes from BCU Course Specification PDF (US0899). Year 1 (Level 4): 5 core modules (120 CATS). Year 2 (Level 5): 3 core (80cr) + 3 optional (pick 40cr). Year 3 (Level 6): 2 core (80cr) + 3 optional (pick 40cr). UCAS code P3W8, institution B25. Course Director: Andy Conway. Fees: UK ~9,790/yr, International ~18,570/yr. Entry: 112 UCAS tariff, IELTS 6.0. BFI partnership. Guardian Top 10. 8 subpages discovered.

[2026-05-12T05:55:00] Extracted Game Programming BSc (Hons) from site_map entry #35 (School of Games, Film and Animation). 3-year BSc with TIGA accreditation. UCAS code 1257, institution B25. Year 1 (Level 4): Game Programming, Modelling, Game Asset Pipeline + 3 inferred modules (120 CATS). Year 2 (Level 5): High Performance Libraries, Game Engine Architecture, Web and Mobile Game Development, Computer Graphics + 2 inferred modules (120 CATS). Year 3 (Level 6): Game Artificial Intelligence, Console Game Development, Individual Honours Project (40cr) + 2 inferred modules (120 CATS). Fees: UK £9,790/yr, International £18,570/yr. Entry: 112 UCAS tariff (BBC), IELTS 6.0. No portfolio required. Optional professional placement year. Unity Academic Alliance, Unreal Academic Partnership. Ranked Top 5 UK for Animation and Games Design (Guardian 2024). BCU course page JS-rendered — module names partially inferred from course description. Data sourced from BCU 2025-26, 2026-27 and Foundation Year pages via Jina reader. 14 subpages discovered.

[2026-05-12T06:06:00] Extracted Design: Active Practice MA from site_map entry #48. Postgraduate course at Birmingham School of Art, Margaret Street (Grade I listed building). Full/Part-time MA with optional 20-week Professional Placement (18 months total). Fees: UK £9,510/yr FT, International £18,970/yr FT, UK £10,460 with placement, International £20,865 with placement. PT: £1,057 per 20 credits (UK), Year 1-80cr, Year 2-100cr. Entry: 2:2 honours degree in Art and Design/Fine Art/Arts-based degree + portfolio. IELTS 6.0 (inferred, no band below 5.5). Key themes: Design Cultures, Connections, Challenges. Social change focus. Interdisciplinary approach blending research and practice. BCU course page is JS-rendered — module details not fully accessible (accordion content). Themes documented from course description. Tags: 可持续设计, 集成设计, 设计战略. Notable alumni projects: Saira Afzal (UN SDG educational game, global competition winner), Deborah De Canio (Zoetrope business, Take That client). 5 subpages discovered. Data quality: high (module details partial).

[2026-05-12T06:13:00] Photography MA (site_map #51) — DEAD LINK. URL https://www.bcu.ac.uk/courses/photography-ma-2026-27 returns 404. Verified all variants (2024-25, 2025-26, 2026-27) — all 404. Web search confirms no standalone Photography MA exists at BCU. Photography is a specialization area within Visual Communication MA (entry #49, already extracted as visual-communication-ma). Site_map entry #51 updated with dead link marker. No data extracted — this entry should be treated as discontinued/merged into Visual Communication MA.

[2026-05-12T06:17:00] Extracted Future Media MA from site_map entry #55. 1-year full-time MA at School of Media, Faculty of Arts, Design and Media. Campus: City Centre Campus (Parkside Building). Programme running since 2012 with proven industry links. MA pathway focuses on strategic/creative roles: client account handling, creative direction, brand development, copywriting, content creation, web analytics, social media monitoring. Fees: International £16,870/yr, UK estimated £9,510/yr. Entry: 2:2 honours degree, IELTS 6.0 (no band below 5.5), 700-1000 word personal statement. No portfolio required. Birmingham Agency Week in semester 1. Small class sizes (~20). Real-world industry briefs throughout. 3-month self-directed Major Project as final assessment. Career paths: Account Manager, Brand Strategist, Creative Producer, Content Curator, Digital Marketing Executive. Data sourced from BCU official page + cross-verified with UniScholars and GyanDhan.
